response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and web engineer with a strong pedigree at Google and Meta, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project — nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no demonstrable Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking systems experience at a technical level, and his database background centers on MongoDB and MySQL rather than the required SQL Server or Oracle. The RIDE Capital role involved investment-adjacent tooling but was built entirely on Node.js, TypeScript, and GraphQL, providing no relevant domain-technical overlap.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals validated by tenures at Google and Meta, signaling high baseline competence and adaptability
Some financial-services context from RIDE Capital (investment platform), offering minimal but non-zero domain adjacency
CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes) partially aligns with optional desirable skills
concerns: C++ experience is limited to ~6 months professionally (NI, 2014–2015) and a thesis project, far below the mandatory 5–10 year requirement
No Windows-platform C++ development history; career is almost entirely Linux, cloud, and web-oriented
No Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking systems experience at the technical or domain level this role demands

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
